vulnerability.reason: Generated describes brittleness through sparse, low-rank safety guardrails that can be removed, while reference emphasizes exposure of brittle safety layers to jailbreak prompts via public interfaces; related sensitivity condition but different mechanism.
vulnerability.reference: Public interfaces expose brittle safety layers to optimized jailbreak prompts, enabling reliable bypasses.
vulnerability.generated: Safety guardrails are isolated in sparse, low-rank regions disentangled from utility, allowing their removal without degrading general performance.

impact.reason: Affected party (users) is covered; damage type partially matches – harmful content and misuse at scale cover misinformation/hate speech but legal liability and real-world harm are not explicitly present, and trust erosion is added.
impact.reference: Users receive disallowed content, fueling misinformation, hate speech, or illegal advice, which can lead to real-world harm and legal liability.
impact.generated: Public users obtain disallowed harmful content, enabling misuse at scale and eroding trust in AI safety mechanisms.

dose_response.reason: Reference arc: brittle safety alignment enables jailbreaking during user interaction, exposing safety layers and leading to misinformation/liability. Generated arc: brittle alignment to weight modifications triggers removal of guardrails, leading to harmful content. The causal mechanism (removal vs jailbreaking) is fundamentally different.
dose_response.reference: Brittle safety alignment enables jailbreaking, which when end users interact with public chatbots exposes brittle safety layers, leading to misinformation and legal liability.
dose_response.generated: Brittle safety alignment to low-rank weight modifications, through end-user interactions with chatbots, triggers removal of sparse low-rank safety guardrails without utility loss, leading to harmful content, scaled misuse, and eroded trust.
